%Text automatically generated by txt2latex
\section{BINARIES}
Download the binaries for your operating system from 
\href{https://github.com/MilanSkocic/codata/releases}{github.com/MilanSkocic/codata/releases.} 
The archives contains 3 folders bin, lib and share that you can simply
copy/paste somewhere on your PATH:
\begin{itemize}
\item Unix-like: \$HOME/.local
\item Windows: add .local to your \%USERPROFILE\% and add it to your PATH
\end{itemize}


\section{SOURCES}
Building the static and shared libraries requires the following dependencies:
\begin{itemize}
\item prep(1) $>$=9.2.0: 20220814
\item gfortran(1) $>$=10
\item gcc(1) $>$=10
\item fpm(1) $>$=0.12
\item python(1) $>$=3.10
\end{itemize}
 
\begin{verbatim}
        $ . ./configure
        $ make sources
        $ make
        $ make install [prefix=your/path]

\end{verbatim}
Building the Python wrapper requires additional dependencies:
\begin{itemize}
\item python interpreter: 3.10, 3.11, 3.12, 3.13, 3.14
\end{itemize}

\begin{verbatim}
        $ make python
  
\end{verbatim}
Install the wheels in the py/sdist/ folder with pip.

Building the documentation requires even more additional dependencies:
\begin{itemize}
\item txt2man(1) $>$=1.7
\item bookman(1) $>$=1.7
\item txt2latex(1) $>$=0.3
\item pdflatex(1) $>$=2025
\item make4ht(1) $>$=0.4c
\item sphinx $>$=8.0
\item ford(1) $>$=7.0
\end{itemize}
